description: Capability declarations for NC1 implementations and consumers.

support_declaration_model.required_rule: All required capabilities must be declared with supported=true.
support_declaration_model.optional_rule: Optional capabilities must be explicitly declared as supported or unsupported.
support_declaration_model.absent_rule: If a non-required capability is absent from declarations, consumers must treat it as unsupported.
support_declaration_model.client_interpretation: Command presence alone does not imply full capability support; capability flags are authoritative.

categories.description: Identity, inventory, and profile self-description surfaces.
Schema-driven setup and activation workflow surfaces.
Client-visible state and commandable mode transitions.
Test catalog discovery and test execution controls.
Published runtime measurements and health signals.
Platform and build identity reporting surfaces.
Service endpoints and transport service declarations.

capabilities.description: Implementation publishes NC1 profile id/version and contract references.
Implementation supports read and update of declared writable operational groups.
Implementation exposes commissioning state and stage progression with validation.
Implementation accepts a global stop request and reports resulting safety state.
Implementation provides a machine-readable test inventory for client rendering.
Implementation supports controlled entry/exit for explicit test mode operations.
Implementation exposes continuous runtime telemetry stream endpoint.
Implementation may expose remote log streaming for diagnostics.

relationships.reason: Commissioning stages edit declared configuration groups.
Test mode transitions can be blocked by active safety stop conditions.
